errorCategories: contrived_distractors: D1 Q3/Q4 have distractors requiring correct computation first then wrong step
repetitive_angles: D2 uses 37° in 4/5 questions — lacks angle variety
difficulty_mismatch: Q15 (D3) is single-concept definitional, really D2
weak_distractors: Q12 distractor A (value=5) is implausibly simple
d5_under_challenging: Q23 area+30-60-90 is more D3-D4 than D5
weak_d1_distractors: Q5 has non-computational distractors (angle as side length, halving hypotenuse, restating stem value) violating distractor rules
d5_under_challenging: Q23 is D3-level (area + 45-45-90 ratios) mislabeled as D5 — same issue as iter1
averaging_distractor_pattern: Q6/Q8 both use 'average the two values' as distractor — not a real student error for right triangles
structural_similarity: Q2/Q4 are near-mirror problems (same 53° angle, inverse setups) reducing batch diversity
trivial_trig_in_d4: Q17 uses tan(45)=1 making the trig component essentially a non-step
difficulty_miscalibration_d3: Q12 and Q14 are single-step D2 problems (choose-the-ratio) mislabeled as D3
unrealistic_scenario: Q12 wheelchair ramp at 53° is physically absurd — violates realistic numbers rule
d5_still_under_challenging: Q22 (altitude-to-hypotenuse decomposition) and Q25 (perimeter + 30-60-90 = area) are D4-level
mirror_image_d2: Q8 and Q10 both use 37°/cos — same ratio solved in opposite directions
angle_over_reuse_d3: D3 batch uses 37° in 3/5 questions (Q11, Q14, Q15), exceeding max 2/5 rule
iterations.convergenceNote: Test avg change iter2→iter3: 5.28→5.25 (0.6% < 2% threshold). Converged. Manual continuation with v1.6.0 (LLM-attention-targeted restructure).
